_comment: Tier-2 Layer-C: skfem Stokes MMS — Taylor-Hood P2/P1 cross-validation of fenics stokes_mms_convergence. Same MMS: ψ = sin(πx)²·sin(πy)², u = curl(ψ), p = sin(πx)·cos(πy), ν = 1, f = -ν·Δu + grad(p) computed analytically. Distinct catalog API: MeshTri.refined + ElementVector(ElementTriP2()) + ElementTriP1() + asm/divergence + scipy.sparse.bmat block + condense+solve. Asserts P2 u L2 <= 5e-4, P1 p L2 <= 2e-2, u EOC in [2.5, 3.5], p EOC in [1.5, 3.0] (Taylor-Hood super-converges on Cartesian meshes). CROSS-BACKEND AGREEMENT: skfem u L2=1.40e-4 vs fenics u L2=1.40e-4 (identical to 5 sig figs); skfem p L2=4.44e-4 vs fenics 4.43e-4 (0.2% diff); EOCs match to 3 sig figs (u: 2.996 vs 2.996; p: 2.636 vs 2.634). The skfem and fenics Stokes implementations differ in EVERY major API surface — mesh, function space, BC enforcement, B-block sign convention, solver dispatch — yet produce the same numerical answer. Caught one sign-convention bug during fixture dev (skfem's divergence has opposite sign from fenics's, requiring explicit negation per the catalog template). Verified on 2026-06-01.
